candidates.why: Component of revenue but not the consolidated top-line; better used only if Total operating revenue were missing.
Component of operating revenue; not the main consolidated top-line measure.
Explicit consolidated revenue line including all revenue components; best representation of total (top-line) revenue.
Expense line, not revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue; not appropriate for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income, not revenue; inappropriate for REVT.
Comprehensive income measure, not revenue; inappropriate for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Represents portion of comprehensive income to minorities, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Total comprehensive income, not revenue.

variables.notes: Total operating revenue is the consolidated top-line including Passenger, Ancillary, Freight, and Other Revenue. No evidence of alternate top-line labels across the preview, so a single priority choice suffices.

candidates.why: Generic bucket that likely aggregates multiple operating cost components; however, it is empty in the preview and less informative than selecting the underlying component lines.
Vague category; may include non-COGS items and appears empty; not ideal as a primary COGS proxy when more specific operating cost components are available.
Direct aircraft maintenance costs; clearly part of direct operating costs (COGS) for an airline.
Likely includes other direct operational costs (e.g., ground handling, etc.). Label suggests it is part of operating COGS rather than SG&A.
Selling and distribution are typically SG&A rather than direct COGS; should instead be treated as overhead, not COGS.
Personnel includes both operational and overhead staff; better classified as SG&A/overhead in this context given separate direct operating cost lines (fuel, maintenance, etc.).
Broad operating overhead bucket; better suited to SG&A than purely direct COGS, but may contain some operational costs.
Net other operating expenses; includes miscellaneous overhead; not clearly direct COGS.
Rental/lease expense can be part of operating costs but often treated separately from core COGS; not chosen when more clearly direct cost lines exist.
Employee benefit cost, typically part of personnel/SG&A rather than direct COGS, especially with more specific direct operating expenses reported.
Direct seasonal operating cost for flights; fits direct COGS for an airline, though no amounts are visible in the preview.
Net of operating income and expenses; a subtotal that mixes revenue and costs, not a pure COGS measure.
Financial, non-operating item; not COGS.
See Rental Expense; generic rental costs, not strictly direct COGS.
Part of aircraft lease cost structure; operational but more of a financing-related lease cost; could be treated as COGS but is more nuanced.
Inventory-related; balance sheet item, not a period cost. Not used directly as COGS.
Liability, not an income statement expense line; should not be used as COGS.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. COGS for an airline corresponds to direct operating costs such as fuel, maintenance, handling, and air traffic charges. The candidate list omits some clear direct-cost rows present in the sheet (Handling Charges, Airport and ATC charges, Aviation Fuel), so these have been added from the full A-column per instructions. I excluded broad/aggregate or overhead-like lines (Operational Expenses, Other Expenses, Personnel expenses, Other Operating Expenses, Operating (Income)/Expense, net) to avoid mixing SG&A and totals with direct COGS. Review is recommended to confirm which operational buckets the user wants treated as direct COGS vs overhead, and whether Additional items like Variable Financial Lease Expenses should also be included. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Handling Charges, [Income Statement] Airport and ATC charges, [Income Statement] Aviation Fuel

candidates.why: Core SG&A-type overhead (airline staff costs, beyond purely direct flight operations). Suitable as a main SG&A component.
Explicit stock-based compensation within SG&A; should be included as an SG&A component per instructions.
Broad overhead bucket (administrative, sales, general). Appropriate SG&A component when not labeled as a total.
Net other operating expenses—likely additional overhead items. Acceptable as part of SG&A components.
Sub-component of personnel-related SG&A; appropriate overhead component when reported separately.
Equity-based compensation; qualifies as SG&A overhead, consistent with instructions to include stock-based compensation.
Personnel-type cost, likely overhead/operating crew; treated as SG&A-style personnel expense rather than direct COGS in this framework.

variables.notes: No explicit single SG&A line exists; instead, multiple overhead components are reported. All selected rows are operating overhead or personnel/stock-compensation related and are not totals, in line with the SG&A_COMPONENTS rules.
No income statement line resembles research & development expense; nothing with R&D, research, or development appears in the A-column. XRD is treated as structurally absent (0).

candidates.why: Primary line for interest expense; negative values indicate cost. Best first-choice mapping for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; an interest expense subtype and valid backup if the main Interest Expense line is unavailable.
Residual portion of interest expense after allocation to specific sub-lines; considered part of interest expense and a valid alternative label.
Represents profit attributable to non-controlling shareholders, not interest; should not be mapped to XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of interest on lease liabilities; valid as a further fallback label for interest expense.
Balance sheet equity component, not an interest expense.
Equity attributable to minority shareholders, not an interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Interest Expense is the main line. The other selected rows are alternative labels or breakdowns of total interest cost. They are ordered so that the most general aggregate is used first, consistent with the XINT priority rule.

candidates.why: Represents equity attributable to shareholders/owners, excluding non-controlling interests; matches the desired BE base. A full series across years is present for this label.
Component of equity, but not the total owners’ equity; should not be used alone as BE.
Part of paid-in capital; again, not the total equity attributable to owners on its own.
Total equity including any non-controlling interests; usable only if no owner-only line existed. Here, a dedicated Shareholders equity line exists, so this is a secondary option.
Technical balancing line, not a substantive equity measure; should be excluded.
Technical balancing line; not appropriate for BE.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing line; not a pure equity measure.
Represents total balance sheet size; not equity alone.

variables.notes: The line 'Shareholders equity' provides equity attributable to owners/parent, excluding non-controlling interests. Although it appears twice in the sheet, the label is identical; per rules we include it once as BE and keep MIB separate to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to non-controlling interests, not the balance sheet stock; not used for MIB stock but confirms presence of minorities.
OCI component related to minorities; not the balance sheet amount of minority interest.
Direct balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; primary mapping for MIB.
Alternate label for non-controlling interests on the balance sheet; valid backup if the other line is missing in some years.
Income statement total minority interest; not the stock measure on the balance sheet.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attributed to minorities; a flow, not the balance sheet amount.

variables.notes: Both 'Non-controlling interests' and 'Minority Interest' appear as equity components on the balance sheet. They are included as alternative labels so later processing can pick the first non-missing value per year. Since BE is mapped to Shareholders equity (owners only), adding these for MIB avoids double counting.
